summary: Molecular sub-classifications of low-grade cerebellar astrocytomas do not translate into significant differences in patient survival or treatment response; their primary role is biologic characterization. Clinical decisions based on standard grading are sufficient for managing most cases without relying on detailed molecular subclasses.
